concept_interdependencies.rationale: Loops and conditionals are tightly coupled - mastering one reinforces the other
Object-oriented programming builds heavily on function concepts (methods)
OOP requires understanding of instance variables and attributes
Functions use variables as parameters and return values
Collections are typically manipulated using loops
Collections use higher-order functions like map, filter, reduce
Logical operators (&&, ||, !) are fundamental to conditional expressions
Loop conditions often use logical operators for complex exit criteria
Operator precedence affects variable assignments and expressions
Precedence matters in function call expressions and return statements
Collections are stored in variables and passed as references
OOP often uses collections for managing object relationships and aggregation
